summary: Fused gate/up QMV + SiLU + elementwise multiply into single specialized kernel (qmv_spec_fused_pair_silu_f16io). Instead of: fused_pair → barrier → silu_elementwise_mul → barrier → down_proj, now: fused_pair_silu → barrier → down_proj. Saves 1 dispatch + 2 barriers per block × 28 blocks = 28 dispatches + 56 barriers per decode token. Result: baseline 182.8 tok/s → ~185-195 tok/s (variable, ~3-7% improvement). p50 latency improved from 5449us to ~5100-5400us.

Tried switching from sequential to concurrent dispatch type (MTLDispatchTypeConcurrent = 1) in beginCompute. Results were noisy (185-196 tok/s) and not clearly better than the fused SiLU baseline (183-195 tok/s). Rolled back. The concurrent dispatch type allows Metal to overlap independent dispatches between barriers, but in practice the QMV kernels dominate each barrier-to-barrier segment so there's little to overlap. Not worth the complexity/risk.
